reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 53 cfs @ Fort Collins , 2 c fs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir Summer is in full swing on the Poudre which means that the bugs have been active and the fish have been too. The book is wide open as far as bug species on the Poudre with species like Caddis, PMDs and Yellow Sallies being the most active right now. Other bugs like Golden Stones and Green Drakes have also been hatching during the right conditions. Although there has been a lot of bug life hatching its worth keeping in mind that the river will be starting to warm up to temperatures where trout start to become very stressed and catching them can be fatal. Fishing earlier in the morning and higher in the canyon can help to avoid these warmer water temps. Dries have been fishing well with bugs consistently coming off in the earlier parts of the day and then becoming active again in the evening. During the middle parts of the day when the bugs slow down dry dropper rigs have been working well with Caddis and PMD droppers. If you have any questions or are interested in booking a guided trip on the Poudre, feel free to give either shop a call. We'll do our best to get you set up for a great day on the water. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year. Or, click the images below for a full list of our current recommended fly patterns.
